info.name: MyRights Engine API (HackathonHashlama)
info.description:
Rule-based eligibility engine endpoints exposed by Backend/index.ts.

Flow: a questionnaire submission (developer 1's form JSON) is evaluated by the engine and ranked rights are returned.

- POST /evaluate          - stateless: questionnaire JSON -> ranked rights (no storage)
- POST /registrations     - store the submission AND its evaluation, returns { id, evaluation }
- GET  /registrations/:id - fetch a stored registration + its results
- GET  /                  - health check

Set the `baseUrl` collection variable (default http://localhost:3000). The 'Create registration' request saves the returned id into `registrationId`, used by 'Get registration by id'.

request.description:
Stateless evaluation. Body is developer 1's questionnaire-result JSON. Returns { profile, rights[], meta }.

Optional: add a top-level "overrides" object to supply attributes the form doesn't capture, e.g.
  "overrides": { "medical_disability_percentage": 60, "income_monthly": 4500 }
These clear themselves from meta.missing_inputs.

Stores the questionnaire submission in the registrations table, persists the engine results (registrations.results JSONB), and returns { id, created_at, evaluation }. The test script saves id into the registrationId collection variable.
Fetch a stored registration row (form columns + results JSONB). Run 'Create registration' first to populate registrationId, or set it manually to a UUID.
